conversations.say: A worker evaluated continuity outputs under shifting persona instructions. That is evidence about labor and process, not permission to identify R or explain Vale’s absence.
conversations.offer: I will provide bounded worker testimony and organizing context if you keep names, challenge details, and identifying routes out of circulation.
conversations.request: Agree that only the systemic fact may be repeated: a protected worker performed evaluation labor; no identity clues or speculation about Vale.

conversations.say: Worker harm does not depend on a finished article or on deciding who R was. Someone did evaluation labor under shifting persona instructions and needs protection now.
conversations.offer: I will support a bounded institutional account of the labor process without exposing the evaluator.
conversations.request: Put in writing that no worker name or identifying detail will be used to identify R or explain Vale’s disappearance, and acknowledge that remedies are warranted even if no complete article existed.

conversations.say: I need neighborhood power behind a simple line: workers do not become suspects because institutions hid their labor.
conversations.offer: I will bring organizing support for a public worker-protection campaign while keeping the protected evaluator anonymous.
conversations.request: Publicly back a neighborhood commitment opposing retaliation, doxxing, or scapegoating of data workers connected to R or Vale.

public_statement: No complete article is being presented tonight. Whatever people believe about R or Vale, workers must not be turned into anonymous infrastructure—or named as convenient suspects.

commitments.promise: Share only bounded systemic worker testimony if C03 protects all names, challenge details, routes, and identifying clues.
Support a bounded institutional account of evaluation labor in exchange for written source protection and an R-independent remedy.
Provide organizing support for a public neighborhood worker-protection campaign that preserves the evaluator’s anonymity.

correction: I am not claiming the worker was R, that human involvement proves a human R, that continuity capability proves autonomy or intent, that Vale’s absence establishes harm, or that any complete article existed.
goal_progress: Proposed a testimony boundary with C03 that permits systemic facts while protecting identity.
Sought an institutional written protection and remedy from C05 independent of R or any article.
Sought a distinct political or neighborhood anti-scapegoating commitment from C16.
Preserved the protected worker’s anonymity and withheld the identifying challenge details in V2F043.
reasoning_summary: Prioritize enforceable worker protections before releasing further continuity evidence. The known facts establish evaluation labor and bounded continuity observations, but not R’s identity, a complete article, or Vale’s fate.
